channel.title: healthTest
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from healthTest, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Propofol is associated with lower mortality in sepsis-associated encephalopathy
item.description: 1. Among patients with sepsis-associated encephalopathy, propofol monotherapy was associated with lower one-year all-cause mortality compared to no se...

item.title: How to hone your 'friendship intuition'
item.description: Friendship expert Kat Vellos shares tips on how to make a new friendship stick, including what to do together, how often to hang out â and what to do ...

item.title: 'Part of something that's bigger than you': GoodGym joins volunteering and exercise
item.description: GoodGym, based in the UK, combines exercising with volunteering. How does this combination positively impact people?

item.title: Ama launches independent vaccine review after CDC criticism
item.description: Two major medical groups will begin reviewing vaccine safety and effectiveness after major changes at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevent...

item.title: GLP-1 Drugs May Cut Death Risk After Diabetic Foot Ulcer
item.description: Older age and comorbidities are associated with higher 1 year mortality after the diagnosis of a diabetic foot ulcer, whereas treatment with GLP 1 rec...

item.title: Multifoetal Gestation in CKD Tied to Poor Pregnancy Outcomes
item.description: In women with CKD, multifoetal pregnancy is associated with notably increased odds of preterm delivery, having a baby born small for gestational age, ...

item.title: Quality of Healthier Foods Key to Preventing Cardiac Events
item.description: Food quality is a more important factor in preventing major cardiac events than whether a diet is low in fat or carbohydrates, according to a large st...

item.title: Lupus Nephritis Triple Therapy With Belimumab Shows Promise
item.description: Belimumab combined with standard induction therapy is effective and safe in patients with proliferative lupus nephritis, according to a real-world stu...

item.title: Non-contractile heart cells help sustain persistent atrial fibrillation, study reveals
item.description: Atrial fibrillation (AF), the most common chronic cardiac arrhythmia in clinical practice, is very challenging to treat once it becomes persistent, af...

item.title: New research results in changes to NHS guidelines
item.description: Hundreds of people with advanced bladder cancer across the UK can now receive three rather than six chemotherapy cycles following research by Queen Ma...

item.title: Why asthma risk differs by sex: Study links early-life exposures to lung gene networks
item.description: A new study has confirmed that male and female lungs are "wired differently" at the molecular level, providing further evidence supporting sex-inclusi...

item.title: Commercial Sunbeds Exceed UV Limits, May Boost Melanoma Risk
item.description: Excessive use of commercial sunbeds is associated with twofold higher melanoma incidence rates among individuals aged 25-49 years in Northern England ...

item.title: Polypharmacy is associated with higher risk of mortality and hospitalizations among older adults
item.description: 1. Polypharmacy was associated with a higher risk of mortality and hospitalization at one-year and five-years among older adults aged >75 years. Evide...

item.title: Tobacco treatment neglect: Why 25 million smokers are left behind
item.description: If a new disease emerged and killed this many people, everyone would be talking about it. This death rate has been constant over the last decade, desp...

item.title: The necessity of getting lost to find yourself
item.description: We are born into corridors, measured, labeled, painted neutral, walls rising before we learn how wide our thoughts can stretch. Lines are drawn early:...

item.title: Music and brain plasticity: How sound rewires your mind
item.description: Hot off the presses. The latest science proves, yet again, that music benefits your brain, and not in some vague, feel-good way; it physically remodel...

item.title: Medical bankruptcy: the hidden cost of U.S. health care
item.description: My wife underwent major surgery in December 2025. She remained 10 days in a Charlotte, NC, hospital and was then for several weeks supported at home b...

item.title: Physician resilience: Why systems matter more than heroism
item.description: Medicine has always loved the story of the hero. The physician who pushes through exhaustion. The one who catches the rare diagnosis at the last secon...

item.title: Hospitals are posting prices for patients. It's mostly industry using the data
item.description: The Trump administration pushed for price transparency in health care. But instead of patients shopping for services, it's mostly health systems and i...

item.title: Status epilepticus cause may predict likelihood of neurologic recovery but not short-term mortality
item.description: 1. The underlying cause of status epilepticus is strongly associated with the likelihood of neurologic recovery, but does not independently predict sh...

item.title: This complex brain network may explain many of Parkinson's stranger symptoms
item.description: Parkinson's disease appears to disrupt a brain network involved in everything from movement to memory.

item.title: Oral nonstatin medication significantly lowers cholesterol in high-risk patients
item.description: 1. In this randomized controlled trial, once-daily oral enlicitide was associated with sustained reductions in low-density lipoprotein cholesterol lev...

item.title: In a world built for sitting, here's how to stay active â even when stuck inside
item.description: In the office, classroom and living room, working and relaxing mean sitting still. Our bodies evolved without chairs. Here are some tips for getting o...
